title: Add test cases for interface array access with loop variable index
body:
## Summary

Adds test cases documenting that Verilator errors when a `for` loop indexes into an interface array with its loop variable:
```systemverilog
simple_if ifaces [3:0] ();

always_comb begin
  for (int i = 0; i < 4; i++) begin
    ifaces[i].value = 8'(i);  // Error: Expecting expression to be constant
  end
end
```

Baseline for #1418 (#1454, #5825, #2788). All tests expect compilation to fail on current master.

### Background

The original version of this PR added an early loop-unrolling pass, but per review feedback this was dropped — it works around the core issue (flattened instantiations can't be indexed at runtime) rather than fixing it. This PR now contains only the test cases as a baseline for a future proper fix.

### Tests

#### `t_interface_array_loop[_noinl]` (expected compile failure)
- Simple ascending/descending loops with all comparison operators (`<`, `<=`, `>`, `>=`)
- Non-unit step (`+= 2`, `-= 2`)
- Multiple interface arrays in the same loop
- Submodule ports with interface array modports (`sink`, `source`, bare)
- Parameterized submodule with overridden `NUM_IFACES`
- Complex index expressions (`N-1-i`)
- Non-zero-based array ranges (`[2:5]`)

#### `t_interface_array_loop_bad` (expected lint failure)
- Runtime-variable loop bound (`i < limit`) correctly produces the existing error
code_blocks:
simple_if ifaces [3:0] ();

always_comb begin
  for (int i = 0; i < 4; i++) begin
    ifaces[i].value = 8'(i);  // Error: Expecting expression to be constant
  end
end
